blocks.content: When they reached the counter, a woman with long hair down to her shoulders greeted them.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: Hello. This is the High Gear user registration page. Are both of you new users?
She looked human, but her face had seams and small bolts symmetrically placed on each side.
It was crude, but perhaps the mechanical look was part of the appeal.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: This friend is new, but I have a user code. Can I register now?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Son of Yolga. East Country.
Shirone turned his head in surprise, and Fermi shrugged.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: Oh, you're a ranker. East Country Rank 7, Son of Yolga. It's an honor to meet such a great person.
Fermi smiled slyly with his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Her acting is top-notch. Then again, with a mechanical face, she probably doesn't need to try hard.
Tutorial manager Magneti.
As someone who managed real-time connections for High Gear, she couldn't be unaware of Fermi's identity.
Magneti manipulated the screen and spoke.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: According to High Gear regulations, a special privilege is activated for Son of Yolga for registering a new user. A 'New Face' bonus of 1 billion galaxies will be awarded.
Shirone glared at Fermi.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: We should take what we can get. Don't worry. I'll use the 1 billion galaxies for you.
Shirone didn't want to get more involved.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Forget it.
Shirone didn't want to get more involved.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I'm not here to play High Gear. You said we could contact the Operator here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You said we could contact the Operator here.
Fermi asked Magneti, 'Can we relay a message to the first designer of High Gear? It's urgent.'

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: Pointing out a specific operator is against regulations. If you have any suggestions, please tell me.
As Fermi seemed ready to back off, Shirone approached Magneti.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It'll only take a moment. If you hear me out, the Operator might change their mind.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: I'm sorry, but I can't approve that. High Gear operators adhere to their own regulations.
Her voice was monotonous, but Shirone noticed her slight delay in response.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: …That's beyond my authority to answer.
You know what situation humanity is in right now. The fate of the world is at stake.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: The fate of the world…
As she pondered those words, Magneti's mechanical face twisted oddly.
Pfft…
And finally, she couldn't hold it in.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: Pahahaha! The fate of the world? That's hilarious! It's even more serious than I thought!
What's so funny?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What's so funny?
As Shirone's expression grew darker with anger, Fermi sighed.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: As expected… In Under Coder, where fakes and anonymity reign, 'sincerity' is just a joke.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I asked what's so funny?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I asked what's so funny?
As Shirone glared at her, Fermi said, 'Calm down. This is a virtual world. No one cares if the real world ends or not.'

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Calm down. This is a virtual world. No one cares if the real world ends or not.
How can you say that? Do you know how many people have died fighting? Is this reality funny to you? No way. You're just running away from reality.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How can you say that? Do you know how many people have died fighting? Is this reality funny to you? No way. You're just running away from reality.
Fermi felt hopeless about how to make him understand.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Sigh. So this place…
At that moment, Magneti's laughter disappeared.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: You can think whatever you want, but insulting an operator can result in penalties. You might be banned from High Gear for a certain period or permanently expelled.
Go ahead and try.
Light from Miracle Stream flared up around Shirone's body.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Would you still laugh if I attacked this place? Would you realize what reality is then?
You can just reconnect.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: You can just reconnect.
Shirone's eyebrow twitched.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But you'd be permanently expelled and never see me again. That's the worst that can happen here. So do as you wish.
Shirone had never revealed his name.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's funny saying this, but I'm so angry I'll play along this time. This isn't reality. You don't know who I am outside. I could be a fighter for justice or the villain you despise.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: And I don't know who you are either. Sure, I've heard your name before. But how would anyone know if you're really saving the world or just enjoying yourself using your power when you wake up from this dream?
I…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I…

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It doesn't matter if it's true or not. The important thing is no one cares here. Under Coder is that kind of place. So please leave reality behind and mind your manners here. Your seriousness is making users uncomfortable.
Fermi nodded repeatedly.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Yeah, that's what I've been saying.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't get it.
Shirone clenched his fists.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Whether it's reality or Under Coder, we're still people talking to each other now. Why ignore that? Your world is being destroyed!

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: …You're really persistent.
Shirone felt frustrated.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What do I need to do to prove it? Will you believe me if I kneel here?

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: Kneel? There are countless people who become dogs and bark or feel like they're being dissected because they want to experience it here. Serial killers in reality take care of the sick here. Don't you get it yet? It's all fake.
Miracle Stream surged up to the ceiling.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then I'll destroy this fake world. Go back and open your window and look outside.
Magneti's hand moved towards the screen.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: …Attacking an operator results in permanent expulsion. You've been warned.
In the tense atmosphere, Shirone lunged at Magneti.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wait…
As Fermi reached out his hand, 'Please.'

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Please.
Shirone knelt on the floor in front of the counter.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I want to save the world. So many people are suffering. I don't know how to convey my sincerity, and maybe you're being deceived by me… But even so…
Even if that's the case.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: Can't you just believe me? Maybe this could save someone, even just one life.
Magneti glared at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: The emotion-filled mechanical face turned cold again.
Sigh.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: What should I tell them?
Shirone looked up in surprise.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Just so you know, even though I'm an operator, I don't have the authority to command the Operator. If it's a strange message, they might not even respond.
Shirone stood up quickly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's fine! Just tell them that Shirone, or rather Yahweh, wants to meet them…

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Tell them Ardino Fermi has a big business proposal. They'll be satisfied.
Then he shrugged at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It's better if I do it since I've dealt with them under my real name. The name Yahweh doesn't mean anything here.
Alright. I've sent it.
Magneti finished sending the message, but after five minutes, there was no response.
As if expecting this, she said, 'Still no response…'
Beep-beep.
At that moment, an alarm message appeared on the screen embedded in the counter.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: They actually responded.
Even she hadn't expected it.
There's a response. I'll play it now.
Get lost.
That's it? That's the end?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's it? That's the end?
As Shirone looked bewildered, Magneti leaned on the counter and said, 'This is unusual too. Under Coder is full of various concepts, but the Operator's personality is genuinely quirky.'

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: This is unusual too. Under Coder is full of various concepts, but the Operator's personality is genuinely quirky.
Fermi agreed.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: If even my name doesn't work, there's no other way. Let's go inside High Gear and find a way.
But they responded.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But they responded.
Shirone didn't give up.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If they really weren't interested, they wouldn't have told us to get lost. Try again.
No, that's…

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: No, that's…
As Shirone nudged him, Fermi raised his hands and moved back to the counter.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Alright, alright.
Fermi looked at Magneti.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Can we send one more message?
Thinking Shirone had a point, she nodded willingly.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: Alright. What should I send?
Hmmm.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I'm thinking of putting a recording device containing data from the outside world up for conditional auction…

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I'm thinking of putting a recording device containing data from the outside world up for conditional auction…
Shirone quickly turned his head.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There are three conditions. First, the bidder must have the ability to decode the record. Second, they must share the viewed data with me. Third, the auction will be on a first-come, first-served basis.
Tapping the screen, Magneti swallowed hard.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: Since we're not strangers, if you're interested, we can make a personal deal. That's all.
Shirone's eyes narrowed as he looked at Fermi.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I knew he'd have a way.
He always needed a push to listen.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I've sent it.
As soon as she finished speaking, a loud bell rang, and Magneti blinked.

blocks.speaker: Magneti
blocks.content: Code name Operator is requesting a voice conversation with operator privileges. Do you accept?
There was no need to ask.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Anyway, it's an enticing offer, right?
Don't get it wrong. I'm connecting because I'm annoyed. Do you think I'd lose in a first-come, first-served auction?

blocks.speaker: Operator
blocks.content: Don't get it wrong. I'm connecting because I'm annoyed. Do you think I'd lose in a first-come, first-served auction?
Actually… that's not confirmed yet.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Actually… that's not confirmed yet.

Where are you selling that? Mentioning an auction means you haven't found a way to decode the recording device. To prevent a drop in value, you chose an extreme competitive method like first-come, first-served, right?

blocks.speaker: Operator
blocks.content: Where are you selling that? Mentioning an auction means you haven't found a way to decode the recording device. To prevent a drop in value, you chose an extreme competitive method like first-come, first-served, right?
Shirone stepped in.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hello, Operator. I'm Shirone. I've heard that in the Abyss…
Shut up. I didn't connect to talk to you.

blocks.speaker: Operator
blocks.content: Shut up. I didn't connect to talk to you.
Shirone felt a surge of anger.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone? Do you think Yahweh joining will change anything? I don't need that recording device.
Fermi smiled with his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Come on, that's not true. Just looking at the items you buy at auctions, I can guess what you're obsessed with.
Let's work together. It's a win-win, right? Just make a program for use in the Abyss.

blocks.speaker: Operator
blocks.content: Let's work together. It's a win-win, right? Just make a program for use in the Abyss.
There was a brief silence.

blocks.speaker: Operator
blocks.content: No. I have to prepare for the Solar War. Aren't you going to? Otherwise, you'll be swallowed by the West Country.
Shirone stepped in again.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Operator, I don't know what the Solar War is, but…
If you don't know, shut up. Why do you keep butting in? And how can you not know about the Solar War? Idiot.

blocks.speaker: Operator
blocks.content: If you don't know, shut up. Why do you keep butting in? And how can you not know about the Solar War? Idiot.
A vein bulged on Shirone's neck.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't know. But is that more important than people's lives?
Haha! You talk well. Do you think you're Yahweh here too? If you're confident, come and find me. If you beat me in a duel, I'll grant any request.

blocks.speaker: Operator
blocks.content: Haha! You talk well. Do you think you're Yahweh here too? If you're confident, come and find me. If you beat me in a duel, I'll grant any request.
Fermi sighed.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I don't like this.
Defeating the Operator, the top ranker in both the West Country and overall, in a one-on-one duel was nearly impossible.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright!
Shirone shouted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You, wait right there! I'll smash High Gear or whatever it is to pieces!
